Genuine L3: pregnant occupational parvovirus contact - check maternal serology FIRST before further escalation (NOT empirical MCA Doppler, NOT amnio, NOT NHIG which doesn't exist for B19). Solid.

verdicts.discard_reason: Duplicates paeds-allergy-derm-011 (scabies in household requiring whole-body permethrin + simultaneous contacts). Drop.

verdicts.fix_sketch: Genuine L3: tinea corporis with confirmatory microscopy + contact sport mat-share - topical terbinafine first-line (NOT TCS which would worsen, NOT oral griseofulvin first-line for skin tinea, NOT antibiotics). Tests calibrated topical/oral choice. Solid.

Genuine L3: SJS/TEN from lamotrigine (mucosal involvement, Nikolsky+, dusky macules with bullae) -> cease drug + burns/HDU + dermatology/ophthal. Distractors test misdiagnosis (EM, SSSS, EM-major with HSV) and unsafe continuation. Solid.

Genuine L3: appropriate first-line urine collection in non-toilet-trained infant -> clean catch (NICE/RCH preferred), NOT bag (false-positive contamination), NOT SPA first-line, NOT catheter unless required. Solid.
Genuine L3: febrile UTI in 8-week-old -> admit + amp+gent + LP (under 3 months, regardless of UTI dx). Tests calibrated age-based workup. Solid.
Genuine L3: bag urine result unreliable for both confirming and excluding UTI -> repeat with clean catch (or catheter if unwell). Tests calibrated interpretation. Solid.

Genuine L3: AKI pattern interpretation - FENa <1%, urine osm >500, urea/Cr ratio raised, recent hypovolaemia = prerenal. Tests biochem interpretation. Distractors are real differentials with their typical patterns. Solid.

verdicts.discard_reason: Duplicates paeds-batch-012 (preschool nephrotic syndrome -> empiric prednisolone, ISKDC regimen). Drop.

verdicts.fix_sketch: Genuine L3: PSGN vs IgAN vs HSP discriminator = low C3 normal C4 (PSGN). Each distractor is feature of one of the differentials. Tests calibrated complement interpretation. Solid.
Genuine L3: HUS refractory hyperK + acidosis + fluid overload -> RRT (NOT continue medical therapy alone, NOT frusemide in anuric, NOT more saline). Tests calibrated escalation threshold. Solid.
Genuine L3: atypical HUS - absent diarrhoeal prodrome + negative STEC + family history. Tests calibrated discriminator. Distractors include features common to both. Solid.

Genuine L3: PSGN - admit + supportive (fluid/salt restriction + antiHTN + penicillin to eradicate carrier). NOT steroid (no role in PSGN), NOT biopsy unless atypical. Distractors test classic over-treatment. Solid.

Lead-in asks paired advice + karyotyping decision. Genuine L3: isolated distal hypospadias with bilateral descended testes = no DSD workup needed but defer circumcision. Tests calibrated DSD threshold. Solid.
Genuine L3: ambiguous/atypical genitalia (penoscrotal hypospadias + bifid scrotum + non-palpable gonad) -> defer sex assignment + karyotype + USS + 17-OHP. Tests recognition of DSD red flags. Solid.

Genuine L3: undescended (or ascended) testis NOT retractile (cannot maintain scrotal position) -> orchidopexy 12-18m. Distractors test under (defer to 4y), over (hormonal), unnecessary imaging. Solid.

Genuine L3: SFU 1-2 antenatal hydronephrosis -> outpatient US day 4 to 6w, no MCUG. Tests calibrated grading-based imaging schedule. Distractors test over (too-early), under (delay 3m), MCUG-everyone. Solid.

Lead-in asks paired dx + management. Genuine L3: torsion of appendix testis (blue dot, upper pole tenderness, normal lie, cremasteric reflex present) -> analgesia and rest, NOT exploration. Distractors test misdiagnosis. Solid.
Lead-in asks paired dx + need for investigation. Genuine L3: laryngomalacia (positional, no red flags) -> no investigation, reassure. Tests calibrated red-flag knowledge. Solid.

verdicts.discard_reason: Sixth bronchiolitis admission-criteria duplicate. Drop.

verdicts.fix_sketch: Correct option quadruples salbutamol + ipratropium + steroid + O2 target. Severe asthma bundle is partially defensible but each is a separate L3 decision. Narrow to single discriminator (ipratropium adds value for severe attack in first hour) or steroid timing (immediate).
Genuine L3: spacer technique correction - one puff at a time + tidal breaths + lip seal. Tests calibrated education. Distractors test other plausible-but-not-the-main-issue corrections. Solid.

Genuine L3: severe bronchiolitis with apnoea + acidosis -> HFNC (NOT immediate intubation in stable-ish child, NOT inadequate low-flow O2, NOT hypertonic+salbutamol). Tests calibrated step on respiratory support ladder. Solid.

Genuine L3: refractory severe asthma after max inhaled + steroid -> IV MgSO4 next (NOT aminophylline first, NOT adrenaline-nebs which is for croup, NOT IV salbutamol bolus before Mg). Tests step on Aus Asthma Handbook ladder. Solid. Some overlap with paeds-batch-018.

verdicts.discard_reason: Duplicates paeds-004, paeds-batch-006, paeds-id-005-l3 (outpatient CAP -> amoxicillin TDS 5 days). Drop.

verdicts.fix_sketch: Genuine L3: procalcitonin most specific for bacterial CAP discriminator over CRP/CXR/RR/symptoms duration. Tests calibrated interpretation. Solid.

Genuine L3: suspected aspirated FB despite normal inspiratory CXR (focal unilateral findings + history) -> insp/exp CXR + urgent rigid bronch. Distractors test classic errors (discharge on normal CXR, treat as asthma, CT before bronch). Solid.
Genuine L3: viral-triggered wheeze in non-atopic child (no interval symptoms, no atopy) -> PRN salbutamol + plan, NOT daily ICS, NOT LTRA. Tests calibrated phenotype-based management. Solid.

Lead-in asks paired Rx + observation. Genuine L3: moderate croup (Westley 6) - nebulised adrenaline + dexamethasone + 4h post-adrenaline observation. Distractors test misdiagnosis (epiglottitis empirical Abx, croup-mistaken-for-asthma, heliox not standard). Solid. Some overlap with paeds-batch-016-l3.

Lead-in asks combination. Genuine L3: <6m pertussis -> admit + azithro + notify + chemoprophylaxis to household. Tests Aus public health protocol. Distractors test under-management. Solid. Some overlap with paeds-id-007.

Correct option triples salbutamol-frequency + preventer-quadruple + GP-24-48h + escalation criteria. Lead-in asks for an action-plan content block which justifies some pairing. Narrow to single high-yield change: temporarily increase preventer at first sign (quadruple ICS / yellow-zone).

Genuine L3: AN with multiple medical instability criteria (HR 38, postural drop, hypothermia, hypoK, hypoPO4, QTc 470, low BMI) -> medical ward admission via MARSIPAN-AU, NOT psych unit first. Tests calibrated dispositional triage. Solid.

Genuine L3: medically stable adolescent AN with supportive parents -> FBT (Maudsley) first-line per RANZCP. Distractors test wrong-modality (CBT-ED alone, olanzapine mono, inpatient when stable, school counsellor). Solid.

Genuine L3: BN with hypokalaemia -> CBT-ED + oral K replacement. Distractors test under-management (SSRI without therapy) and over-management (admit, olanzapine, topiramate). Solid. Some overlap with paeds-adol-011.
Genuine L3: BED + multiple failed CBT-ED courses -> lisdexamfetamine (the only TGA-registered stimulant for adult BED). Tests calibrated drug-of-choice knowledge. Solid.

Genuine L3: classic acid-base pattern of self-induced vomiting (low Cl, high HCO3, urinary Cl <20). Distractors test alternative mechanisms (RTA, diuretic abuse with HIGH urine Cl, primary hyperaldosteronism with HTN, laxative abuse). Solid.
Genuine L3: ARFID - food restriction WITHOUT weight/shape concern, sensory-driven. Distractors test misclassification (AN, picky-eating, phobia, pica). Solid.

Correct option triples BDD-CBT-with-ERP + SSRI-higher-dose-range + 12-16 week trial. Narrow to single discriminator (most appropriate pharmacological choice OR most appropriate psychological modality). Distractors test classic errors (refer for more surgery, BZD-only, reassurance). The dose-range-and-duration point is specific to BDD.

Genuine L3: postpartum psychosis (day 8, mania features + AH + persecutory + sleep<2h) -> urgent admission, ideally mother-baby unit. Distractors test wrong-management (treat as baby blues, outpatient SSRI, community team delay, ED-discharge). Tests recognition of high-risk timing. Solid.

Genuine L3: EPDS 14 + item 10 = 1 (low risk) - structured interview to confirm dx + first-line therapy + consider SSRI per COPE. Distractors test under (rescreen) and over (ED for item 10 'hardly ever', empirical venlafaxine, defer screening). Solid.

Lead-in asks paired SSRI + reasoning. Genuine L3: sertraline first-line in lactation (low milk transfer, no infant accumulation). Distractors test classic confusions (fluoxetine has long half-life and active metabolite, citalopram QTc concerns, venlafaxine SNRI, doxepin contraindicated). Solid.

Genuine L3: neonatal adaptation syndrome from late-trimester SSRI. Distractors test sepsis (excluded by negative workup), opioid withdrawal (no exposure), hypoG (within range), HIE (good Apgars/no acidosis). Solid.

Correct option quintuples pregnancy test + counsel + LARC + TGA form + review-indication. Narrow to single discriminator (most important next step under TGA program: contraception adequacy + risk acknowledgement). Distractors test continue-unchanged, abrupt-cease, switch-blind, tubal-ligation. The PPP framework knowledge is the L3 point.

Genuine L3: MDD with melancholic features (diurnal variation, EMW, weight loss, psychomotor retardation, mood non-reactivity). Distractors test misclassification (dysthymia, adjustment, bipolar). Tests calibrated specifier application. Solid.

Genuine L3: SSRI choice in young adult (sertraline preferred for tolerability, sister-response, sexual side-effects no worse than other SSRIs but better profile overall vs paroxetine/venlafaxine/mirtazapine). Tests calibrated discrimination on drug-specific tradeoffs. Solid.

Correct option triples cease serotonergics + BZD + cooling + admit. Lead-in asks initial step. Narrow to single discriminator (cease serotonergic + benzodiazepine sedation) - the dantrolene distractor is for NMS not SS, which is the key teaching point.

Genuine L3: TRD after 2 adequate trials -> augment with aripiprazole (TGA-approved indication). Distractors test classic errors (third SSRI/SNRI, dangerous combos (MAOI + SNRI), abrupt MAOI switch without washout, St John's wort). Solid.

Genuine L3: ECT first-line for psychotic MDD with food refusal. Distractors test classic misconceptions (MI absolute contraindication - it isn't, capacity bars lawful Rx - SDM can consent in most jurisdictions, AD cessation before ECT not required, cognitive screen needed). Solid.

Genuine L3: hypomanic episodes (4+ days, observable change, no hospitalisation, no psychosis, no marked impairment) + depressive episodes = BP2. Distractors test misclassification (BP1 needs mania, cyclothymia needs sub-threshold, MDD misses hypomania, BPD has different pattern). Solid.

Genuine L3: acute mania in known BP1 with prior lithium response -> olanzapine + restart lithium combination (NOT sertraline/diazepam, NOT lithium-mono in acute mania, NOT valproate-mono first in known-responder, NOT lamotrigine which is for maintenance/depression). Solid.
Genuine L3: pre-lithium baseline = FBC, UEC/eGFR, Ca, TFTs, betahCG, ECG. Tests calibrated workup. Distractors test wrong-tests or incomplete. Solid.

verdicts.discard_reason: Duplicates psych-eating-perinatal-011 (EPDS 14 + COPE pathway -> structured interview + psych therapy first-line + SSRI if needed). Drop.

verdicts.fix_sketch: Correct option triples SSRI + CBT + 2wk review + 6wk response. Lead-in asks plan. Narrow to single discriminator: combined pharm+psych for moderate-severe GAD-7 OR specific 2-week activation review. Distractors are good (BZD prolonged, CBT-alone with delay, mirtazapine, no Rx).

Genuine L3: panic disorder with agoraphobic avoidance after thorough exclusion of medical causes (ECG, troponin, metanephrines, Holter, TTE, TSH). Tests calibrated dx after appropriate workup. Distractors are real differentials all ruled out. Solid.
Genuine L3: dual dx OCD + PTSD - tests recognition that ego-dystonic rituals + trauma-specific intrusive symptoms warrant both diagnoses. Distractors test mono-dx forcing. Solid.

Correct option pairs TF-CBT/EMDR + AUD management. Lead-in is fine for paired focus. Narrow to single modality choice (TF-CBT or EMDR over SSRI first-line, NOT critical incident debriefing which can harm, NOT BZD-PRN). Tests PTSD evidence base.

Genuine L3: performance-only anxiety -> propranolol PRN (NOT BZD due to performance impairment, NOT daily SSRI for situational, NOT quetiapine, NOT CBD). Tests specific anxiety subtype management. Solid.

Genuine L3: SSRI/SNRI discontinuation syndrome (FINISH: flu-like, insomnia, nausea, imbalance, sensory disturbance, hyperarousal) - restart + gradual taper. Distractors test misdx (relapse vs discontinuation, meningitis, gastro, dose-up). Solid.
Genuine L3: classic melancholic features specifier - tests knowledge of specific DSM-5 criteria over alternative interpretations. Solid.

Genuine L3: BPD vs MDD - splitting/idealisation-devaluation pattern is the specific discriminator. Each distractor is a feature but not the specific discriminator. Tests calibrated DSM-5 application. Solid.

Genuine L3: BPD first-line = structured psychological therapy (DBT/MBT) - NOT pharm-mono (no drug has TGA approval for BPD core symptoms), NOT another SSRI, NOT routine admission. Tests calibrated evidence-base knowledge. Solid.
Genuine L3: ASPD requires conduct disorder before age 15 (DSM-5). Tests specific criterion. Distractors are features but not the discriminator. Solid.

Correct option is a 50-word paragraph bundling NPD diagnosis + discriminators from ASPD AND histrionic. Lead-in asks paired dx + key discriminator. Narrow to single discriminator pair (NPD vs ASPD: no childhood conduct disorder). The triple-comparison is too wide.
Genuine L3: OCPD vs OCD discriminator = ego-syntonic vs ego-dystonic. Tests specific DSM-5 differentiator. Distractors are features common to both. Solid.
Genuine L3: delirium dx = CAM criteria (acute onset+fluctuation+inattention+disorganised/altered consciousness). Distractors test misdiagnosis pathways (MMSE alone, MoCA, GCS, GDS). Solid.
Genuine L3: dementia vs delirium vs depression discriminator = gradual progression without acute fluctuation. Distractors test features common across diagnoses. Solid.

Genuine L3 anti-pattern: BPSD with grimacing on transfers + recent constipation -> treat unrecognised pain FIRST + environmental measures, NOT antipsychotic/BZD reflexively. Tests calibrated stepwise approach. Solid.

Genuine L3: persistent distressing psychosis after non-pharm trial -> low-dose risperidone with 12-week review. Tests calibrated dosing + scheduled review for antipsychotic in dementia (stroke/death risk). Distractors test wrong-dose, wrong-drug, indefinite use. Solid.

Genuine L3: DLB with distressing hallucinations -> rivastigmine (NOT antipsychotic - severe sensitivity, NOT levodopa - worsens psychosis, NOT BZD). Tests calibrated DLB-specific drug avoidance. Solid.
Genuine L3: MCI (cognitive complaint + objective deficit + preserved IADLs). Tests specific dx threshold. Distractors test mislabelling as normal/dementia/specific subtype. Solid.

Genuine L3: elderly depression with hypoNa + falls risk + CKD -> sertraline at LOW dose with sodium and falls monitoring. Distractors test classic errors (amitriptyline anticholinergic falls risk, high citalopram QTc, high venlafaxine, mirtazapine anticholinergic + sedating falls). Solid.

Genuine L3: IPT specifically has strongest perinatal evidence base (role-transition focus). Distractors test wrong-modality (ACT, EMDR/DBT not perinatal-first-line). Tests COPE guideline specifics. Solid.

Correct option is a 60-word paragraph bundling stage + MI + FRAMES + offer pharmacotherapy + indication rationale. Lead-in 'stage + intervention plan' partly justifies. Narrow to single discriminator (stage = Contemplation OR most appropriate intervention = MI + offer of pharmacotherapy). The combined-disorder/AUDIT-22 dose threshold for pharm offer is the L3 point.

Genuine L3: recurrent depression with characterological/relational patterns post-CBT -> psychodynamic psychotherapy as next-step modality. Tests calibrated therapy choice for chronicity + relational issues. Solid.

Genuine L3: FEP -> EPPIC/headspace early-psychosis service, NOT solo GP prescribing, NOT misdx as cannabis-induced (cannabis was 4w ago), NOT delay for MRI/EEG, NOT immediate involuntary if voluntary engagement possible. Solid.

Genuine L3: metabolic burden + need for low-sedation morning dose -> aripiprazole (low weight gain, low sedation, OD AM dosing). Distractors test metabolic-burden drugs (olanzapine continued/clozapine), wrong-frequency (risperidone BD, quetiapine nocte). Solid.
Genuine L3: acute dystonia -> IM anticholinergic (benztropine), NOT BZD-only (won't treat), NOT propranolol (akathisia not dystonia), NOT dantrolene (MH not dystonia). Tests specific Rx. Solid.

Genuine L3: akathisia from risperidone with emergent SI -> reduce dose + propranolol (NOT anticholinergic which is for dystonia/EPSE, NOT increase lithium, NOT clozapine, NOT wait-and-see). Tests differentiation from dystonia/parkinsonism. Solid.
Genuine L3: NMS vs SS discriminator = recent D2 antagonist + lead-pipe rigidity + hyporeflexia (vs SS hyperreflexia + clonus + serotonergic drug). Distractors are features common to both. Solid.

Genuine L3: treatment-resistant schizophrenia (2 adequate antipsychotic trials failed) -> clozapine. Tests calibrated TRS threshold. Distractors test wrong-strategy (3rd trial, dual-antipsychotic, depot, ECT). Solid.

Genuine L3: clozapine-induced constipation/ileus + iatrogenic loperamide for overflow -> cease loperamide + admit + decompress + escalated laxatives + surgical review (life-threatening). Tests recognition of clozapine GI hypomotility risk. Solid.

Genuine L3: Wernicke encephalopathy after dextrose without thiamine -> IV B-complex (Pabrinex) high-dose + Mg correction + then continue dextrose. Tests calibrated dosing (IM 100mg insufficient for established WE). Solid.

Genuine L3: AUD maintenance Rx choice - naltrexone for craving reduction. Tests calibrated discrimination (acamprosate for abstinence-maintenance not craving, disulfiram avoided given upcoming wedding, BZD wrong, topiramate not first-line). Solid.

Genuine L3: disulfiram contraindicated in IHD (severe vasomotor response can precipitate cardiac event). Distractors test misclassification (Child-Pugh A doesnt contraindicate, age alone, statin/metoprolol not contraindications). Solid.

Genuine L3: TD on FGA -> switch to SGA (ideally clozapine which has lowest TD risk and may improve TD). NOT increase haloperidol (worsens), NOT add risperidone, NOT anticholinergic (often worsens TD), NOT abrupt cessation. Tests calibrated TD management. Solid.

Genuine L3: clozapine break >48h -> retitrate from 12.5mg per protocol (cardiovascular + seizure risk). Tests specific protocol. Distractors test classic errors (restart usual dose, halve, switch, add valproate). Solid.

Genuine L3: 12-week metabolic monitoring schedule for SGA - anthropometry + BP + fasting BSL/HbA1c + lipids. Tests calibrated guideline content. Distractors test under (weight only), over (OGTT, ABPM), defer. Solid.

Correct option quadruples continue + lifestyle + metformin + cardiometabolic ref + 4-6 week panel. Narrow to single discriminator (add metformin OR continue with lifestyle modification - calibrated risk vs benefit when clozapine is working). Distractors are good (stop and switch, halve dose, defer review, urgent echo).
Genuine L3: lithium toxicity prevention - avoid NSAIDs (including COX-2), avoid abruptly switching maintenance for acute pain, paracetamol is safe choice with hydration advice for hot weather. Solid.
Genuine L3: lithium neurotoxicity signature = cerebellar + hyperreflexia + rising level + falling renal function (vs SS clonus, NMS rigidity, fever-as-cause). Tests calibrated differentiation. Solid.

Correct option triples switch-discussion + LARC + annual review + signed acknowledgement + switch-before-conception. Narrow to single discriminator (most appropriate immediate action under PPP framework: switch discussion OR ensure highly effective contraception). Some overlap with psych-eating-perinatal-014-l3.

Genuine L3: specific risk figures for valproate (10% major malformations, 30-40% neurodev impairment) - tests calibrated counselling. Distractors test understating, restricting-trimester misconception, folate-eliminates-risk myth, reversibility myth. Solid.

Genuine L3: TCA overdose with QRS widening + acidosis -> sodium bicarbonate. Distractors test wrong-Rx (flumazenil contraindicated post-seizure, flecainide worsens sodium channel block, charcoal too late, magnesium for unrelated arrhythmia). Solid.

Genuine L3 anti-pattern: rural ED TCA overdose - call Poisons Information Centre for case-specific advice. Tests safety procedural knowledge over reflex acting. Distractors test classic errors (wait for GP info, discharge on QRS<120, empirical intralipid). Solid.

Lead-in asks for paired classification + dose + monitoring + registry action. Genuine L3: clozapine amber zone (ANC 1.5-1.99) -> continue + twice weekly monitoring + notify registry. Distractors test misclassification (green/red zones, defer, dangerous augmentation). Solid.

verdicts.discard_reason: Duplicates psych-psych-sub-039 (clozapine break >48h -> retitrate from 12.5 mg per protocol). Drop.

verdicts.fix_sketch: Correct option is a 60-word paragraph explaining mechanism + immediate management + dose adjustment plan. Lead-in pairs mechanism + management. Narrow to single discriminator: most appropriate immediate action (stop sertraline + HCT, fluid restrict). Mirtazapine-alternative could be standalone.

Genuine L3: SSD vs IAD - somatic symptom + excessive thoughts/feelings/behaviours. Distractors test misclassification (IAD has minimal somatic symptoms, GAD different focus, FND has neurological symptoms, factitious is conscious fabrication). Solid.
Genuine L3: FND - positive Hoover's sign as rule-in diagnosis + FND-specific physiotherapy. Tests anti-pattern of empirical Rx for other conditions. Solid.

Genuine L3: IAD - minimal somatic symptoms but preoccupation with serious illness despite repeated reassurance. Distractors test SSD (would have prominent somatic symptoms), GAD (broader worry), OCD (different obsession theme), delusional (lacks insight). Solid.
Genuine L3: factitious disorder vs malingering = no external incentive (factitious motivated by sick-role) vs financial/legal gain (malingering). Tests calibrated DSM-5 application. Solid.
Genuine L3: subclinical hypothyroidism mimic of depression - test TSH/T4/TPO before further psychotropic adjustment. Tests calibrated workup sequencing. Solid.

Correct option quadruples antibody panel + MRI + neurology + empirical aciclovir. Narrow to single discriminator (most appropriate dx workup = anti-NMDA Abs OR most appropriate empirical Rx = aciclovir). The anti-NMDA encephalitis recognition is the L3 point. Distractors test misdx (NMS without antipsychotic exposure, more antipsychotic, paraneoplastic-first).

Correct option lists 6+ deprescribing/management actions. Lead-in asks for initial plan. Narrow to single discriminator (deprescribe deliriogenic medications first, OR do not treat asymptomatic pyuria). Distractors test classic errors (add antipsychotic, treat AC asymptomatic UTI, psych ward transfer).
Genuine L3: TRD per RANZCP CPG -> lithium augmentation. Distractors test wrong-strategy (third SSRI mono, non-PBS combos, BZD anxiolytic, premature ECT). Solid.
Genuine L3: fluoxetine -> MAOI requires 5-week washout (vs 2 weeks for other SSRIs) due to norfluoxetine half-life. Tests specific PK reasoning. Solid.

Lead-in asks paired toxidrome + discriminator. Genuine L3: Hunter criteria SS - inducible clonus + hyperreflexia + serotonergic exposure (including linezolid as MAOI). Distractors test wrong-toxidromes (NMS, anticholinergic, sympathomimetic, MH) with their typical features. Solid.

Genuine L3: severe lithium toxicity (level 2.1 + AKI + ataxia + hyperreflexia) -> cease lithium + withdraw NSAID/ACE + IV fluids + consider haemodialysis. Distractors test classic errors (continue lithium, charcoal, frusemide). Solid.

Genuine L3: preconception bipolar maintenance - add quetiapine for breakthrough mania-predominant pattern (avoid valproate teratogenicity, avoid lamotrigine mono for mania-predominant, avoid abrupt lithium cessation). Tests calibrated drug choice. Solid.

Correct option is 50-word paragraph bundling IV bicarbonate + target QRS + pH + threshold criteria. Lead-in pairs intervention + threshold. Narrow to single discriminator (most appropriate immediate intervention with appropriate threshold marker). Some overlap with psych-psychopharm-009.

Correct option pairs dx + immediate response + ongoing taper. Lead-in pairs cause + response. Narrow to single discriminator (most likely dx = discontinuation syndrome vs recurrence, OR most appropriate immediate response = resume + slower taper). The venlafaxine-half-life rationale is the L3 point. Some overlap with psych-mood-018.

Genuine L3: most important MODIFIABLE acute risk factor = means restriction (firearm). Tests calibrated risk stratification - other factors (Aboriginal identity, brother, past attempt) are non-modifiable, hopelessness less acutely modifiable. Solid.

Correct option lists 6 components of Stanley-Brown safety plan + shared copy + same-week DBT contact. Lead-in asks single next step. Narrow to single discriminator (Stanley-Brown collaborative safety planning, NOT no-suicide contract which has no evidence). Distractors test classic errors (involuntary admit when engaged, ineffective contract, antipsychotic, wrong-service).

Correct option pairs SSRI + psychotherapy + rehab engagement. Narrow to single discriminator (sertraline at low dose - safest post-stroke SSRI; mirtazapine+amitriptyline distractor good for poor combo). Some elements legitimately go together. Borderline keep.

Correct option quadruples sertraline + psych therapy + grief support + weekly review. Narrow to single discriminator (most appropriate first-line treatment = SSRI + perinatal-informed therapy). Bereavement-specific elements could be standalone.

Genuine L3: high-risk adolescent with specific plan + means + timing + recent bereaved + minority stress -> admit (voluntary or involuntary) + identity-affirming supports. Distractors test classic dangerous errors (discharge with safety plan when imminent risk, ED-only SSRI start, CP notification wrong threshold, dismiss). Solid.

Genuine L3: vegan + PPI + macrocytic anaemia + neuropsych features + glossitis + ataxia + lemon-yellow + hypersegmented PMNs -> B12 + functional markers (MMA, homocysteine) + IF antibodies. Tests calibrated workup before psychotropic. Solid.
